cards.title: WARD EXPERIENCE #001
cards.subtitle: The First Patient I Ever Touched

content.story: The gloves felt thinner than I expected... (FULL TEXT HERE)
content.psychological_insight: Fear of harming patients is normal. Confidence develops through repetition, not imagination.

content.story: Before consultants arrived, the ward felt alive... (FULL TEXT HERE)
clinical_lessons: Ward rounds test thinking under pressure
Observation skills matter as much as memorization
Senior nurses often prevent avoidable mistakes
content.psychological_insight: Clinical confidence is not loudness. It is calm accuracy.

cards.title: WARD EXPERIENCE #003

clinical_lessons: Small lapses can create contamination risks
content.psychological_insight: Embarrassment in training is temporary. Unsafe habits are not.
